title: Brent Cusher
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Brent Cusher
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: I am Associate Professor of Leadership Studies at CNU, where I teach all courses in the LDSP curriculum, as well as courses in the Honors program.
education_entries: Ph D in Political Theory, University of Toronto (Ontario)
BA in Political Science, Carleton College
research_interests: Classical Models of Leadership, Statesmanship, Rhetoric and Persuasion, Leadership Ethics, Legal Philosophy, Ancient and Modern Political Philosophy
teaching_interests: Leadership, especially through the lenses of history, moral philosophy, and political philosophy.
publications: Encyclopedia Article (2023). Foundational Lawgiving . SAGE Publishing. Volume, 1. Pages, 369-73.
Encyclopedia Article Foundational Lawgiving . SAGE Publishing.
Book, Scholarly-New (2021). Philosophy and Leadership: Three Classical Models and Cases . Routledge Press.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2020). Thucydides on the Innovative Leadership and its Limits: Pericles of Athens . The Political Science Reviewer. Volume, XLIV. Issue, 2. Pages, 26.

Book, Scholarly-New (2018). Leadership and the Unmasking of Authenticity: The Philosophy of Self-Knowledge and Deception . Edward Elgar Publishing (series: New Horizons in Leadership Studies). Pages, 250 pp..
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2016). Wisdom and the Art of Persuasion: Rousseau's Platonic Teaching on the Virtuous Legislator . University of Toronto Press.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2015). Leaders in Conversation: The Dialectic Model of Leadership Education in Plutarch’s Lives . Journal of Leadership Education/Association of Leadership Educators. Volume, 14. Issue, 2. Pages, 198-208.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2015). ’Gods of Vengeance and Compassion:’ The Withering Criticism of Compassion in Cormac McCarthy’s Blood Meridian . Lexington Books.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2014). Cormac McCarthy’s Definition of Evil: Blood Meridian and the Case of Judge Holden . Perspectives on Political Science/Taylor and Francis. Volume, 43. Issue, 4. Pages, 223-230.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2014). How does Law Rule? Plato on Habit, Political Education, and the Art of Legislation . Journal of Politics/Cambridge University Press.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2013). From Natural Catastrophe to the Human Catastrophe: Plato on the Origins of Law . Law, Culture and the Humanities/Sage. Volume, 9. Issue, 2. Pages, 20.
